ogTitle: Cabarita vs Cabbage Tree Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cabarita and Cabbage Tree Island.
heading: Cabarita vs Cabbage Tree Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cabarita, New South Wales and Cabbage Tree Island, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cabarita has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Cabbage Tree Island (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cabarita or Cabbage Tree Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cabarita has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Cabbage Tree Island scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cabarita scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Cabbage Tree Island scores 20/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cabarita or Cabbage Tree Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cabarita has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.07% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
